title: Franz Joseph I of Austria becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Franz Joseph I or Francis Joseph I was Emperor of Austria, King of Hungary, and the ruler of the other states of the Habsburg monarchy from 2 December 1848 until his death in 1916.

title: Maria Theresa of Austria becomes Apostolic Queen of Hungary
fact: Maria Theresa was the ruler of the Habsburg monarchy from 1740 until her death in 1780, and the only woman to hold the position in her own right.

title: Charles I of Austria becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Charles I and IV was Emperor of Austria, King of Hungary, and the ruler of the other states of the Habsburg monarchy from November 1916 until the monarchy was abolished in November 1918.

title: Francis II, Holy Roman Emperor becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Francis II and I was the last Holy Roman Emperor as Francis II from 1792 to 1806, and the first Emperor of Austria as Francis I from 1804 to 1835.

title: Sigismund becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Sigismund of Luxembourg was Holy Roman Emperor from 1433 until his death in 1437.

title: Joseph II, Holy Roman Emperor becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Joseph II was Holy Roman Emperor from 18 August 1765 and sole ruler of the Habsburg monarchy from 29 November 1780 until his death.

title: Leopold I, Holy Roman Emperor becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Leopold I was Holy Roman Emperor, King of Germany, King of Hungary, Croatia, and Bohemia.

title: Ferdinand I, Holy Roman Emperor becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Ferdinand I was Holy Roman Emperor from 1556, King of Bohemia, Hungary, and Croatia from 1526, and Archduke of Austria from 1521 until his death in 1564.

title: Leopold II, Holy Roman Emperor becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Leopold II was the penultimate Holy Roman Emperor, as well as King of Hungary, Croatia and Bohemia, and Archduke of Austria from 1790 to 1792, and Grand Duke of Tuscany from 1765 to 1790.

title: Ferdinand I of Austria becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Ferdinand I was Emperor of Austria from March 1835 until his abdication in December 1848.

title: Charles VI, Holy Roman Emperor becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Charles VI was Holy Roman Emperor and ruler of the Austrian Habsburg monarchy from 1711 until his death, succeeding his elder brother, Joseph I.

title: Matthias Corvinus becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Matthias Corvinus was King of Hungary and Croatia from 1458 to 1490, as Matthias I.

title: Frederick III, Holy Roman Emperor becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Frederick III was Holy Roman emperor from 1452 until his death in 1493.

title: Ferdinand II, Holy Roman Emperor becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Ferdinand II was Holy Roman Emperor, King of Bohemia, Hungary, and Croatia from 1619 until his death in 1637.

title: Emperor Rudolf II becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Rudolf II was Holy Roman Emperor (1576–1612), King of Hungary and Croatia, King of Bohemia (1575–1608/1611) and Archduke of Austria (1576–1608).

title: Ferdinand III, Holy Roman Emperor becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Ferdinand III was Archduke of Austria, King of Hungary and Croatia from 1625, King of Bohemia from 1627 and Holy Roman Emperor from 1637 to his death.

title: Maximilian II, Holy Roman Emperor becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Maximilian II was Holy Roman Emperor from 1564 until his death in 1576.

title: Joseph I, Holy Roman Emperor becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Joseph I was Holy Roman Emperor and ruler of the Habsburg monarchy from 1705 until his death in 1711.

title: Stephen I of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Stephen I, also known as King Saint Stephen, was the last grand prince of the Hungarians between 997 and 1000 or 1001, and the first king of Hungary from 1000 or 1001 until his death in 1038.

title: Matthias, Holy Roman Emperor becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Matthias was Holy Roman Emperor from 1612 to 1619, Archduke of Austria from 1608 to 1619, King of Hungary and Croatia from 1608 to 1618 and King of Bohemia from 1611 to 1617.

title: Albert II of Germany becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Albert the Magnanimous, elected King of the Romans as Albert II, was a member of the House of Habsburg.

title: Władysław III of Poland becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Władysław III of Poland, also known as Ladislaus of Varna, was King of Poland and Supreme Duke of the Grand Duchy of Lithuania from 1434, as well as King of Hungary and Croatia as Vladislaus I from 1440 until his presumed death at the Battle of Varna.

title: Ladislaus the Posthumous becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Ladislaus V, more commonly known as Ladislaus the Posthumous, was Duke of Austria and King of Hungary, Croatia and Bohemia.

title: Louis II of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Louis II was King of Hungary, Croatia and Bohemia from 1516 to 1526.

title: Mary I of Hungary becomes Apostolic Queen of Hungary
fact: Mary, also known as Maria of Anjou, reigned as Queen of Hungary and Croatia between 1382 and 1385, and from 1386 until her death.

title: Charles I of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Charles I, also known as Charles Robert (Hungarian: Károly Róbert; Croatian: Karlo Robert; Slovak: Karol Róbert; Italian: Caroberto;, was King of Hungary and Croatia from 1308 to his death.

title: Béla IV of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Béla IV was King of Hungary and Croatia between 1235 and 1270, and Duke of Styria from 1254 to 1258.

title: Vladislaus II of Bohemia and Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Vladislaus II, also known as Vladislav, Władysław or Wladislas ;, was King of Bohemia from 1471 to 1516 and King of Hungary and King of Croatia from 1490 to 1516.

title: Wenceslaus III of Bohemia becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Wenceslaus III was King of Hungary and Croatia from 1301 to 1305, and King of Bohemia and Poland from 1305.

title: Andrew II of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Andrew II, also known as Andrew of Jerusalem, was King of Hungary and Croatia between 1205 and 1235.

title: Ladislaus I of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Ladislaus I, also known as Saint Ladislas, was King of Hungary from 1077 and King of Croatia from 1091.

title: John Zápolya becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: John Zápolya or Szapolyai, was King of Hungary from 1526 to 1540.

title: Ladislaus IV of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Ladislaus IV, also known as Ladislaus the Cuman, was King of Hungary and Croatia from 1272 to 1290.

title: Charles III of Naples becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Charles III of Naples, also called Charles the Small or Charles of Durazzo, was King of Naples and the titular King of Jerusalem from 1382 to 1386 as Charles III, and also King of Hungary from 1385 to 1386 as Charles II.

title: Andrew III of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Andrew III the Venetian was King of Hungary and Croatia between 1290 and 1301.

title: Béla I of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Béla I the Boxer or the Wisent was King of Hungary from 1060 until his death.

title: Stephen V of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Stephen V was King of Hungary and Croatia between 1270 and 1272, and Duke of Styria from 1258 to 1260.

title: Gabriel Bethlen becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Gabriel Bethlen was Prince of Transylvania from 1613 to 1629 and Duke of Opole from 1622 to 1625.

title: Ferdinand IV, King of the Romans becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Ferdinand IV was made and crowned King of Bohemia in 1646, King of Hungary and Croatia in 1647, and King of the Romans on 31 May 1653.

title: Andrew I of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Andrew I the White or the Catholic was King of Hungary from 1046 to 1060.

title: Coloman becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Coloman the Learned, also the Book-Lover or the Bookish, was King of Hungary from 1095 and King of Croatia from 1097 until his death.

title: Géza I of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Géza I was King of Hungary from 1074 until his death.

title: Solomon becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Solomon, also Salomon was King of Hungary from 1063.

title: Géza II of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Géza II was King of Hungary and Croatia from 1141 to 1162.

title: Béla II of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Béla II the Blind was King of Hungary and Croatia from 1131 to 1141.

title: Stephen II of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Stephen II, King of Hungary and Croatia, ruled from 1116 until 1131.

title: Otto V or III, Duke of Bavaria becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Otto III, a member of the Wittelsbach dynasty, was the Duke of Lower Bavaria from 1290 to 1312 and the King of Hungary and Croatia between 1305 and 1307.

title: Samuel Aba becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Samuel Aba reigned as King of Hungary between 1041 and 1044.

title: Peter, King of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Peter, known as Peter Orseolo or Peter the Venetian, was King of Hungary twice.

title: Stephen III of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Stephen III was King of Hungary and Croatia between 1162 and 1172.

title: Emeric, King of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Emeric, also known as Henry or Imre, was King of Hungary and Croatia between 1196 and 1204.

title: Ladislaus III of Hungary becomes Apostolic King of Hungary
fact: Ladislaus III was King of Hungary and Croatia between 1204 and 1205.
